# Flaim Fantasy

> Flaim connects your ESPN, Yahoo, and Sleeper fantasy leagues to Flaim Fantasy in ChatGPT, with optional manual MCP clients also available for read-only, league-specific analysis using the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

## What Flaim Is

Flaim is a free, open-source MCP server behind a live ChatGPT-first fantasy analysis experience. It gives AI assistants read-only access to your real fantasy sports data and combines live league data with built-in fantasy analyst guidance so answers are grounded in your league — not generic rankings.

Single MCP endpoint: https://api.flaim.app/mcp

## MCP Tools (9 total)

| Tool | What it does |
|------|-------------|
| get_user_session | Lists all connected leagues across all platforms |
| get_ancient_history | Historical leagues and seasons (2+ years old) |
| get_league_info | League settings, scoring format, and members |
| get_roster | Team roster with player stats and lineup slots |
| get_matchups | Weekly matchups and scores |
| get_standings | League standings and rankings |
| get_free_agents | Available free agents; ESPN/Yahoo include ownership percentages, Sleeper returns identities |
| get_players | Player lookup with ownership context |
| get_transactions | Recent adds, drops, waivers, and trades |

## Supported Platforms and Sports

| Platform | Sports | Auth Method |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| ESPN | Football, Baseball, Basketball, Hockey | Chrome extension required for private ESPN leagues |
| Yahoo | Football, Baseball, Basketball, Hockey | OAuth 2.0 (sign in with Yahoo) |
| Sleeper | Football, Basketball | Username only (public API) |

## How to Connect

1. Create a Flaim account at https://flaim.app
2. Go to https://flaim.app/leagues
3. Connect your fantasy platforms there:
   - ESPN: Install the Chrome extension to sync private ESPN leagues
   - Yahoo: Start Yahoo auth from /leagues and wait for league discovery
   - Sleeper: Enter your exact username and wait for league discovery
4. Open Flaim Fantasy in ChatGPT
5. Authorize Flaim when prompted
6. Start a fresh chat and ask "What leagues do I have?" to confirm setup

Optional manual MCP clients can use the same server URL after leagues are connected: https://api.flaim.app/mcp

## Example Prompts

- "Who should I start this week?"
- "Who's on the waiver wire?"
- "Show me the standings"
- "What trades happened this week?"
- "Who are the best available QBs?"
- "Compare my roster to my opponent's"
- "What fantasy leagues do I have?"
- "How has my team done over the last 3 weeks?"

## FAQs

### What does Flaim do?
Flaim is a ChatGPT-first fantasy analysis app, a skill that teaches AI how to analyze your league, and a connector to data from your actual fantasy leagues. It lets you ask about your actual roster, matchup, standings, transactions, available free agents, waiver wire, and more.

### Is my data safe?
Flaim is read-only. It cannot trade, drop, or modify anything in your league. ESPN credentials are encrypted and stored securely — they are never shared with AI providers.

### Why does ESPN need a Chrome extension?
ESPN doesn't offer a public API for private fantasy league data. For private ESPN leagues, the Chrome extension syncs the session details Flaim needs for read-only access.

### How does Flaim connect to AI clients?
Flaim Fantasy is available in ChatGPT. Optional manual MCP clients can also add the single server URL, and Flaim handles authentication and data retrieval after your leagues are linked in /leagues.

### What AI assistants work with Flaim?
The primary experience is Flaim Fantasy in ChatGPT. Optional manual MCP clients can also connect to the same endpoint when they support remote HTTP MCP and OAuth, including Claude, Perplexity, and Gemini CLI. Claude uses custom connectors, Perplexity uses custom remote connectors, and Gemini CLI uses terminal MCP server configuration.
